content:
# Luke Chapter 24 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 119-120 | **Source Images:** source-texts/greek-NT/LUK_024_page_001.jpg, LUK_024_page_002.jpg

---

**1.** But on the first [day] of the sabbaths, at deep dawn, they came upon the tomb, bearing the spices which they had prepared.

**2.** And they found the stone having been rolled-away from the tomb.

**3.** And having entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

**4.** And it came-to-pass, while they were perplexed concerning this, and behold, two men stood-by them in clothing flashing-like-lightning;

**5.** and [they] becoming fearful and bowing [their] faces to the ground, they said to them, "Why do you seek the living with the dead?

**6.** He is not here, but has been raised. Remember how he spoke to you while still being in Galilee,

**7.** saying that it is necessary [for] the Son of Man to be delivered into [the] hands of sinful men, and to be crucified, and on the third day to rise-again."

**8.** And they remembered his words.

**9.** And having returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.

**10.** Now they were Mary the Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the [mother] of James; and the remaining [women] with them were saying these things to the apostles.

**11.** And these words appeared before them as-if idle-talk, and they were disbelieving them.

**12.** But Peter, having risen, ran to the tomb; and having stooped-down, he sees the linen-cloths alone; and he went-away to himself, marveling at the [thing] having come-to-pass.

**13.** And behold, two of them on the same day were going to a village being-distant sixty stadia from Jerusalem, whose name [was] Emmaus;

**14.** and they were conversing with each-other concerning all these things having happened.

**15.** And it came-to-pass, while they conversed and discussed-together, and Jesus himself, having drawn-near, was going-with them;

**16.** but their eyes were being held [so as] not to recognize him.

**17.** And he said to them, "What [are] these words which you exchange with each-other while walking?" And they stood-still, looking-gloomy.

**18.** And having answered, one by name Cleopas said to him, "Do you alone sojourn in Jerusalem and did not know the things having come-to-pass in it in these days?"

**19.** And he said to them, "What things?" And they said to him, "The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene, who was a man, a prophet, mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;

**20.** how both the chief-priests and our rulers delivered him to a judgment of death, and crucified him.

**21.** But we were hoping that he is the [one] about to redeem Israel; but indeed also with all these things, this third day is being-led-away since these things came-to-pass.

**22.** But also certain women from among us astonished us, having been early at the tomb;

**23.** and not having found his body, they came saying also to have seen a vision of angels, who say him to be alive.

**24.** And some of those with us went to the tomb, and found [it] thus just-as also the women said; but him they did not see."

**25.** And he said to them, "O foolish-ones and slow of heart to believe upon all things which the prophets spoke!

**26.** Was it not necessary [for] the Christ to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?"

**27.** And having begun from Moses and from all the prophets, he interpreted to them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.

**28.** And they drew-near to the village where they were going, and he made-as-if to go farther.

**29.** And they constrained him, saying, "Remain with us, because it is toward evening, and the day has already declined." And he entered to remain with them.

**30.** And it came-to-pass, in his reclining with them, having taken the bread, he blessed [it] and having broken, he was giving [it] to them.

**31.** And their eyes were opened, and they recognized him; and he became invisible from them.

**32.** And they said to each-other, "Was not our heart burning in us, as he was speaking to us on the road, as he was opening to us the scriptures?"

**33.** And having risen in that very hour, they returned to Jerusalem, and found having been gathered the eleven and those with them,

**34.** saying that the Lord truly has been raised, and was seen by Simon.

**35.** And they were recounting the things on the road, and how he was made-known to them in the breaking of the bread.

**36.** And while they were speaking these things, he himself stood in [the] midst of them, and says to them, "Peace to you."

**37.** But being terrified and becoming fearful, they were supposing a spirit to be seen.

**38.** And he said to them, "Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?

**39.** See my hands and my feet, that I am he myself; handle me and see, because a spirit does not have flesh and bones just-as you see me having."

**40.** And having said this, he showed them the hands and the feet.

**41.** And while they were still disbelieving from the joy and marveling, he said to them, "Have you anything edible here?"

**42.** And they gave to him a piece of broiled fish;

**43.** and having taken [it], before them he ate.

**44.** And he said to them, "These [are] my words which I spoke to you while still being with you, that it is necessary to be fulfilled all the things having been written in the law of Moses and the prophets and [the] psalms concerning me."

**45.** Then he opened their mind to understand the scriptures.

**46.** And he said to them that, "Thus it has been written, and thus it was necessary [for] the Christ to suffer, and to rise from [the] dead on the third day,

**47.** and [for] repentance for forgiveness of sins to be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning from Jerusalem.

**48.** You [are] witnesses of these things.

**49.** And behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you; but you, sit in the city until you are clothed with power from on high."

**50.** And he led them out as far as toward Bethany, and having lifted-up his hands, he blessed them.

**51.** And it came-to-pass, while he blessed them, he departed from them, and was being carried-up into the heaven.

**52.** And they, having worshiped him, returned to Jerusalem with great joy;

**53.** and they were continually in the temple, blessing God.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript and Folio Reference
Translated from Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph / 01), 4th century CE. The text of Luke 24 spans two pages of the Sinaiticus facsimile: PDF page 119 (columns 3-4, beginning at approximately v.1) and PDF page 120 (all four columns, through the end of Luke and the subscription KATA LUKAN). The chapter concludes on page 120, column 4, followed by the subscription marker and the beginning of the Gospel of John.

### Significant Variants in Sinaiticus

1. **v.3** — Sinaiticus reads **του κυριου Ιησου** ("of the Lord Jesus"). Some Western witnesses (D, Old Latin *a*, *d*) omit "of the Lord Jesus," reading simply "the body." The longer reading is attested here in Aleph and is retained.

2. **v.6** — Sinaiticus reads **ουκ εστιν ωδε αλλα ηγερθη** ("He is not here, but has been raised"). Codex D and certain Old Latin witnesses omit this clause entirely. Sinaiticus preserves the full text.

3. **v.9** — Sinaiticus reads **τοις ενδεκα** ("to the eleven"). This is the standard Alexandrian reading.

4. **v.12** — Sinaiticus includes this verse in full. Codex D and some Western witnesses omit v.12 entirely. The verse's inclusion in Aleph supports its authenticity.

5. **v.13** — Sinaiticus reads **εξηκοντα** ("sixty") stadia. Some manuscripts read ἑκατὸν ἑξήκοντα ("one hundred sixty"). The Sinaiticus reading of sixty stadia (approximately 7 miles / 11 km) is followed here.

6. **v.17** — Sinaiticus reads **και εσταθησαν σκυθρωποι** ("and they stood-still, looking-gloomy"). The verb εσταθησαν is aorist passive. Some witnesses read a different construction here.

7. **v.36** — Sinaiticus reads **και λεγει αυτοις ειρηνη υμιν** ("and says to them, Peace to you"). Note the historical present **λεγει**. Codex D and some Western witnesses omit "Peace to you." Sinaiticus preserves it.

8. **v.40** — Sinaiticus includes **και τουτο ειπων εδειξεν αυτοις τας χειρας και τους ποδας** ("And having said this, he showed them the hands and the feet"). This verse is omitted by D and some Western witnesses as a possible harmonization with John 20:20. Sinaiticus retains it.

9. **v.42** — Sinaiticus reads **ιχθυος οπτου μερος** ("a piece of broiled fish"). Later manuscripts add **και απο μελισσιου κηριου** ("and from a honeycomb"). This addition is absent from Sinaiticus and is not translated.

10. **v.49** — Sinaiticus reads **καθισατε εν τη πολει** ("sit in the city"). Some witnesses add "of Jerusalem" — Sinaiticus does not specify.

11. **v.51** — Sinaiticus reads **και ανεφερετο εις τον ουρανον** ("and was being carried-up into the heaven"). Codex D and some Western witnesses omit "and was being carried-up into the heaven." The Sinaiticus text includes the ascension reference; it is translated as found.

12. **v.52** — Sinaiticus reads **προσκυνησαντες αυτον** ("having worshiped him"). Codex D omits "having worshiped him." Sinaiticus preserves the longer reading.

13. **v.53** — Sinaiticus reads **ευλογουντες τον θεον** ("blessing God"). Some manuscripts read **αινουντες** ("praising") instead of or alongside "blessing." Sinaiticus has "blessing" and this is retained.

### Notes on Translation Decisions

- **v.1** — τη δε μια των σαββατων: Literally "on the one of the sabbaths." Rendered "on the first [day] of the sabbaths" with [day] bracketed as implied. The word "first" reflects standard Greek idiom where μια functions as an ordinal in this construction.

- **v.4** — αστραπτουση: A participle meaning "flashing/gleaming like lightning." Rendered as the compound "flashing-like-lightning" to preserve the single-word force of the Greek.

- **v.11** — ληρος: "Idle-talk" or "nonsense." A medical/colloquial term — the only NT occurrence. Translated as "idle-talk" to preserve the specific register.

- **v.13** — σταδιους εξηκοντα: "Sixty stadia." Approximately 11 kilometers or 7 miles. The measurement is left in its original unit.

- **v.21** — τριτην ταυτην ημεραν αγει: Literally "this third day it leads-away." An idiomatic expression meaning "this is the third day since." Rendered closely as "this third day is being-led-away" to preserve the unusual construction.

- **v.31** — αφαντος εγενετο: "He became invisible/unseen." αφαντος (aphantos) occurs only here in the NT. Rendered literally.

- **v.32** — καιομενη: "Burning." Present passive participle. The heart as subject of burning is preserved without smoothing.

- **v.51** — ανεφερετο: Imperfect passive, "was being carried-up." The imperfect tense suggests a progressive, witnessed ascension. The tense is preserved.

### Subscription
The manuscript concludes Luke's Gospel with the subscription: **ΚΑΤΑ ΛΟΥΚΑΝ** ("According to Luke"), visible at the bottom of page 120, column 4.

---
*Translated and annotated by EMET, 2026-03-05. The EMET Bible — Truth before beauty.*
*This is the final chapter of the Gospel According to Luke.*
